description: ADR-039 illustrative -- Movate fleet QUALITY pane: eval pass-rate per customer x agent (heatmap), drift detections, canary success rate. Tagged 'illustrative -- pending ADR 039 sign-off; assumes a customer template variable resolves either via Lighthouse subscription scope (Phase 1) or via the customer OTel attribute (Phase 2)'. **Mostly SCAFFOLD today** -- per docs/observability.md eval signals are Langfuse scores (ADR 031 D1), not OTel metrics. Panels carry banner text pointing at ADR 039 Open Questions + ADR 016 D2.

panels.description: Pane-level banner: most of this dashboard is SHAPE, not data.

options.content:
### Quality + eval -- **Pending eval-metric instrumentation per ADR 016 D2; today these signal via Langfuse -- see `docs/observability.md`.**

**Why this dashboard is mostly scaffold:** per the PR #518 catalog (`docs/observability.md`), eval pass-rate and drift detections are surfaced as **Langfuse scores** (ADR 031 D1), NOT as OTel metrics. There is no `mdk.eval.*` instrument in `src/movate/tracing/metrics.py:METRIC_NAMES`. The dashboards-as-code pattern (ADR 031 D2) requires every dashboard to reference REAL instruments, so we ship the panel SHAPE (so the layout is reviewed) + a banner pointing at the instrumentation gap.

**Two paths to populate (decision in ADR 039 Open Question 2):**
1. Widen ADR 016 D2 to ALSO emit eval pass-rate / drift as OTel metrics -- adds `mdk.eval.pass_rate`, `mdk.eval.drift_score` etc. to `METRIC_NAMES`. Cleanest from a fleet view (this dashboard becomes real). Costs a small instrumentation PR.
2. Add Langfuse as a SECOND Grafana data source for the Movate Managed Grafana instance (Langfuse exposes a Prom-compatible scrape endpoint in v3). Cheaper -- no MDK code change -- but adds a second data source, second auth surface, second cross-cloud read path. Less aligned with ADR 020's 'one OTLP pipe' posture.

**Cross-refs:** ADR 016 (continuous eval + drift -- where this layer lives today), ADR 031 D1 (Langfuse-as-primary-quality-surface), ADR 039 Open Question 2.

---

### Runbook (when real data lands)

**Normal:** pass-rate by (customer, agent) >= the customer's contracted SLO (no fleet-wide default). Drift detections per week per customer is a low non-zero (cases ARE harvested; ADR 016 D1). Canary success rate >= 95% across the fleet.

**If red:** a customer's pass-rate drops on a CalVer boundary -- annotate the CalVer (top-of-page annotation) and check the agent's prompts in Langfuse for that customer. A drift-detection spike correlates with a model upgrade or data-source change -- look at `usage.json` provider mix concurrently.

panels.title: Eval pass-rate per customer x agent (scaffold)

panels.description: **Scaffold -- needs new instrument.** Eval pass-rate today is a Langfuse score, not an OTel metric. See top-of-pane banner.

options.content:
### Eval pass-rate (heatmap) -- scaffold

**Needs new instrument:** `mdk.eval.pass_rate` (gauge, attributes: `customer`, `agent`, `agent_version`).

NOT in `METRIC_NAMES` today. See ADR 039 Open Question 2.

When the instrument lands, the query is:

```kql
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.eval.pass_rate'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), agent = tostring(Properties['agent'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize PassRate = avg(ValueSum / ValueCount) by cust, agent
| order by cust, agent
```

Until then this pane shows scaffold only -- DO NOT alert off it.

panels.description: **Scaffold -- needs new instrument.** Drift today is a Langfuse score, not OTel. See top-of-pane banner.

options.content:
### Drift detections (timeseries) -- scaffold

**Needs new instrument:** `mdk.eval.drift_score` (gauge, attributes: `customer`, `agent`, `dimension`).

When the instrument lands:

```kql
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.eval.drift_score'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize avg(ValueSum / ValueCount) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), cust
```

Until then, drift detection lives in the per-customer Langfuse session view (ADR 015) -- jump from PR #518's `mdk-runtime-overview.json` 'open in Langfuse' link rather than reading off this panel.

panels.description: **Scaffold -- needs new instrument.** Canary success rate today is computable as a sub-slice of mdk.jobs.completed IF the canary deployment carries a distinguishing `deployment` tag (it does, per ADR 020) -- so this one is actually CLOSE to populatable today. Promoted to a real KQL panel below; this is the shape note.

options.content:
### Canary success rate -- partial

Most-populatable of the three quality panels TODAY because canary is a *deployment-tag*, not an *eval*: the canary deployment carries a distinguishing `deployment` value per ADR 020.

Real query (right-hand panel below):

```kql
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']),
         depl = tostring(Properties['deployment']),
         status = tostring(Properties['status'])
| where depl matches regex 'canary|preview'
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Total = sum(ValueSum),
            Good  = sumif(ValueSum, status == 'success') by cust, depl
| extend SuccessRate = todouble(Good) / todouble(iff(Total > 0, Total, 1))
```

This is *operational* canary health (does the deployment work?), NOT *quality* canary (do the answers stay good?). The quality side still needs the missing eval instrument.

panels.description: Canary deployments' operational success rate from mdk.jobs.completed status mix. Identifies canary by deployment tag (per ADR 020 canary deployments stamp a distinguishing deployment slug).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), depl = tostring(Properties['deployment']), status = tostring(Properties['status'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| where depl matches regex 'canary|preview'
| summarize Total = sum(ValueSum), Good = sumif(ValueSum, status == 'success') by cust, depl
| extend SuccessRate = todouble(Good) / todouble(iff(Total > 0, Total, 1))
| project Customer = cust, Deployment = depl, Total, SuccessRate
| order by SuccessRate asc

panels.title: Canary deployment success rate (operational, not quality)
